name: Winterbourne Earls
overview: Winterbourne Earls is a village in the Bourne valley on the A338, about 3.5 miles north-east of Salisbury. It forms part of the civil parish of Winterbourne, created in 1934 by amalgamating the three adjoining villages of Winterbourne Earls, Winterbourne Dauntsey and Winterbourne Gunner (the parish covers around 1,000 residents). The flint church of St Michael and All Angels, built in 1867-68 to serve Earls and Dauntsey, stands in the village, which also has the area's primary school and Glebe Hall.
character: A settled, contiguous Bourne-valley village that runs almost seamlessly into Winterbourne Dauntsey, with a strong, active local community and chalk-stream surroundings.
amenities: Winterbourne Earls Church of England Primary School
St Michael and All Angels Church
The Winterbourne Arms pub at adjoining Winterbourne Dauntsey
Active community groups across the Winterbournes (theatre, clubs and events)

schools.name: Winterbourne Earls Church of England Primary School

commutes.notes: About 3.5 miles south-west on the A338 for shops, services and the station
From Salisbury on South Western Railway; fastest services about 1h 20m
Regular buses run along the A338 Bourne valley corridor

prices.summary: Housing ranges from period cottages to mid-20th-century and modern family homes, giving more variety and volume than the smaller valley hamlets; demand is supported by the village primary and the short Salisbury commute. See the live Rightmove sold-price page for current figures.

thingsToDo: Walking along the River Bourne and the surrounding chalk downland
Following the Roman Port Way on the higher ground towards Old Sarum
Community events at Glebe Hall and across the Winterbournes
placesToEat: Cafe and refreshment events run by the Bourne Valley community groups
pros: Its own Good-rated village primary school
Closest of the Bourne valley villages to Salisbury, about 3.5 miles
cons: Limited shopping in the village - main shopping is in Salisbury
The A338 carries through-traffic past the village
Some properties sit close to the Bourne flood corridor
whoItSuits: Families wanting a sociable, well-connected valley village with a good primary school and a short hop into Salisbury.

sources.label: Ofsted - Winterbourne Earls CE Primary (URN 151736, Good, May 2024)
Wiltshire OPC Project - Winterbourne Dauntsey (St Michael & All Angels history)
